div.head: 概述
div.p: 83號窟是南崖F區倒數第二個刻經窟（）。此處續刻了D區66號窟未完成的《大般涅槃經》（下稱《涅槃經》），但僅在d壁銘刻19行，在該品的一半處戛然而止。此爲該窟唯一一處刻經。

div.head: 位置

list.item: 位於南崖F區。高於現今步道約7米。

div.p: 83號窟與85號窟一并組成F區一個四連窟組合中上排的雙窟。兩窟上方有一窄檐，伸出岩壁約25至40厘米。83號窟底距81號窟頂120厘米，85號窟底距84號窟頂140厘米。
兩窟在後部逐漸靠攏，窟口間的距離約80厘米寬。而在後部，兩窟間墻體厚度縮减爲40厘米。

div.head: 形制
div.p: 敞口標準方形窟。雙層窟口爲卧佛院中保存最好的例子之一。西側雙層寬及進深皆爲18厘米；東側寬爲16厘米，進深爲5至12厘米。窟底平面呈梯形，由前向後逐漸拓寬（）。後部，d壁稍向右偏。窟頂由前向後擡升。

list.item: d壁：前端高213厘米、後端高227厘米，寬286厘米。
f壁：前端高216厘米、後端高224厘米，寬298厘米。

figure.head: 83號窟平面圖。雷魯卡根據馬本漢和王平國田野繪圖製作
Ground plan of cave 83; drawing by Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

div.head: 改造
div.p: 距窟口25厘米、f壁18厘米處，有一長方形門沿，爲近期所鑿，大小爲51×14厘米，深3厘米。地面有一開裂，距f壁23厘米處并與之平行，從後壁延伸至凹槽處。凹槽外有一處三角形開口延伸向外，積水由此流出洞口。此開口與下方墻體兩道對角斜向的凹槽之一相連，鑿痕則原爲安置82號龕外檐之用。
d壁距窟口32厘米處，有一縱向凹槽，高77厘米，寬約7至8厘米，今人開鑿，爲安置門框之用，破壞了第8至11行的刻經。f壁相應有一凹槽，距離窟口約20厘米，同樣寬約7至8厘米，但向窟頂方向延伸。在窟頂以下該凹槽直角轉彎延伸向窟内，此横向凹槽大小爲14×33厘米。
距離窟口約32厘米的窟頂有兩處長方形孔洞，其中一處近d壁窟口，大小爲11×6厘米；另一處距d壁83厘米，大小爲14×6厘米。墻壁及窟頂有深淺不一的粗鑿痕。
最顯著的變化在於窟的後部。f壁有一窗口聯通相鄰的第85窟。此窗位於e壁後沿，距窟底116厘米處，大小爲108×83厘米。

list.item: 打磨面，大小爲190×170至185厘米，僅於d壁前部。其位置距窟口9厘米，距窟頂12厘米，距窟底24厘米，距後方e壁110至128厘米。打磨面的底部有粗略斜向鑿痕；窟頂與打磨面間有較細緻的斜向鑿痕。d壁後方布滿幾處平行刻痕，走向不一，1至4厘米寬。其末梢切入墻體前端的打磨面。因此可見，打磨面或曾一度延伸至後壁，但後來墻體的後半部分重新被磨糙。
有一細窄的刻經面，180×49厘米，距窟頂12至15厘米，距窟口約12厘米，距窟底23厘米，距後方e壁約233厘米。上下沿近乎水平。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 良好；壁面平整。第一行幾不可見。前七行下方風化。窟面以上約90至110厘米處亦風化。
窟頂下方約10至25厘米處，有一處横向區域，或由於化學藥水處理不當之故，墻體剥落。
距窟口約4至6厘米處，有并排垂直的四個方形孔洞，長、寬約4至5厘米。

div.head: 佛經標識

row.cell: ［大般涅槃經聖行品之］二 十二：今漫漶不清，拓片上仍可見三字，其餘根據59號窟d壁第76行復原。標識《大般涅槃經》第十二卷的開始，亦是第七品《聖行品》第二部分的開始。
……五盛陰苦能生：標識《大般涅槃經》第十二卷第七品《聖行品》第二部分的結束。刻經至此突然中斷。

div.p: 兩處塗刻“果”字；大小分别爲10×8.5厘米和8.5×7厘米，刻痕很深，分别距窟底123厘米和125厘米。分别距離末行刻經13厘米和38厘米。

div.head: e壁
div.p: e壁的鑿痕較d壁的鑿痕更具條理性，下方的綫條統一向右傾斜，上方的綫條統一向左傾斜。
f壁後方與d壁後方有同樣的粗糙鑿痕。窟頂情况相同，後部亦與前部不同。現場考察可知，全窟後部鑿痕爲前部刻經完成後添加。
窟底以上100厘米處，距窟口8厘米處，有一不規則打磨面，尺幅爲15×8厘米，有一後刻題記“王寶龕”，共1行，9.8×3.7厘米，其中“寶”字已被刻意磨損。刻字尺幅：“王”，1.1×1.5厘米；“龕”，3.2×3.7厘米。

div.head: 討論
div.bibl:
彭家勝第8頁
彭家勝提供該窟測量數據，體積爲12.255立方米。作者指出經目不詳。他的以上信息由胡文和提供。

胡文和第10頁
給出的該窟尺寸的測量數據同彭家勝的研究。作者同樣認爲經目不詳，但指出經文尚存十行。

胡文和第68頁
胡文和沿用此前出版物的看法，見第10頁。

李良和鄧之金第44頁
李良和鄧之金提供該窟測量數據，提出e壁及f壁有刻經。該文還提供d壁刻經面的測量數據，但未辨經目。

《成都考古發現》第392－393頁、第408頁
該文提供石窟的測量數據，并詳細描述建築形制。d壁刻經起初被誤作《金光明經》。但文末《安岳卧佛寺經窟統計表》指出18行刻經出自《大般涅槃經》第十二卷第七品《聖行品》之二，并説明此刻經未完成。

李琛妍第41頁、第50頁、第74頁註28
李琛妍將83窟納入她所分的第VI組（本卷F區）予以討論。作者認爲該壁經文爲《維摩詰所説經》第十品至第十四品，其餘諸品刻於85號窟。

曹丹和于春第13－14頁
曹丹和于春提供各窟壁及d壁刻經面的測量數據。該文指出，窟壁因風化而難以辨認經文，經文僅存18行，每行91字，共1638字。

秦臻、張雪芬和雷玉華第106－107頁、第125頁、 第437－439頁
作者再次采用成都文物考古研究所的測量數據（），確認刻經爲《大般涅槃經》。同時，該文移録全部刻經，并用其方法系統標示經文本。

肥田路美第110頁
肥田路美描述了83號窟的位置。作者辨識d壁有刻經，但無法辨明經目。作者還提到e壁和f壁有打磨面，但無刻經。

鄧星亮第79頁、第89頁
鄧星亮提供了測量數據，并總體描述該窟。作者提及d壁有刻經。刻經出自《大般涅槃經》第十二卷，尚存20行。作者移録了起始和結尾的經文。

div.head: Overview
div.p: Cave 83 in section F is the penultimate cave with sutra texts in the southern escarpment (fig. ). The Great Parinirvāṇa Sutra ( Mahā­pari­nirvāṇa-sūtra , Da banniepan jing 大般涅槃經, hereafter: Nirvana Sutra) was here taken up again, continuing where the carvers had left off in cave 66 in section D; but only 19 columns were engraved on wall d, stopping midway through the chapter. This is the only text in the cave.

div.head: Location

list.item: Southern slope, section F, ca. 7 meters above the walkway.
Direction of wall d: 1˚ (front), 15˚ (rear); wall f: 2˚ (front), between 359˚ and 12˚ (rear).

div.p: The twin caves 83 and 85 form the upper row in the ensemble of four caves in section F. The two are sheltered above by a narrow ledge in the rock face that protrudes some 25–40 cm. The floor of 83 is 120 cm above the ceiling of cave 81, and the floor of 85 is 140 cm above cave 84.
The two caves are angled towards each other inside the mountain. At the front, the wall between them is 80 cm wide, but at the rear only 40 cm.

div.head: Shape
div.p: Box-shaped with standard-sized open front. The reveal at the top of the entrance is one of the best preserved in the Grove. On the right (west): width and depth 18 cm; on the left (east): width 16 cm and depth 5 to 12 cm. The trapezoidal ground plan, deeper than it is wide, widens towards the rear (fig. ). Towards the rear, wall d curves slightly to the right; the ceiling rises towards the rear.

list.item: Wall d: height at the front 213 cm, at the rear 227 cm; width 286 cm.
Wall e: height at midpoint 225 cm; width 212 cm.
Wall f: height at the front 216 cm, at the rear 224 cm; width 298 cm.

div.head: Changes
div.p: About 25 cm from the entrance of the cave and about 18 cm from wall f is a rectangular trough, recently cut into the floor, measuring 51 × 14 cm, and 3 cm deep. A crack in the floor, parallel to and at a distance of some 23 cm from wall f, leads from the rear of the cave into the trough, and a triangular-shaped cut in the front wall of the trough leads to the exterior, so that water can flow out of the cave. The cut feeds into one of the two diagonal slits in the wall below, which originally served to anchor a roof that sheltered niche 82.

About 32 cm from the entrance, a vertical slit, measuring 77 cm high and ca. 7–8 cm wide, was also recently cut into wall d to hold the frame of a door, damaging columns 8 to 11 of the sutra text. A corresponding slit for the frame was cut into wall f, ca. 20 cm from the entrance, and again 7–8 cm wide, but in this case running all the way to the ceiling. Directly below the ceiling, the slit makes a right-angle turn and extends into the cave, this horizontal portion measuring 14 × 33 cm.

There are two rectangular holes in the ceiling some 32 cm from the entrance; one, 11 × 6 cm, is at the corner with wall d, and the other, 14 × 6 cm, begins 83 cm from wall d. Different degrees of scoring are found on the walls and ceiling.

The most damaging change is found in the rear of the cave, where a window was cut in wall f to connect the cave with neighboring cave 85. The window, which borders wall e, is 116 cm from the floor and measures 108 × 83 cm.

list.item: A polished area, 190 × 170–185 cm, covers only the front part of wall d. It begins 9 cm from the entrance opening and 12 cm below the ceiling; it ends ca. 24 cm above the floor and 110 to 128 cm from the rear wall e. The base below the polished area shows rough diagonal striations; the narrow area below the ceiling and above the polished field shows smaller and finer diagonal striations. The rear part of wall d is covered with patches of parallel striations at a variety of angles, the striations 1–4 cm wide. The tips of these striations cut into the polished area at the front of the wall; it would seem, therefore, that the polishing once extended all the way to the rear, but that the rear half of the wall was later roughened again.

A narrow field of script, 180 × 49 cm, begins 12–15 cm below the ceiling, and 12 cm from the entrance; it ends 23 cm above the floor and 233 cm from wall e. The upper and the lower edges are approximately horizontal.

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Moderate; the surface is even; the first column is hardly visible. The first 7 columns are worn in the lower part. An area from ca. 90 to 110 cm above the floor is also worn.
A horizontal area, ca. 10–25 cm below the upper edge, is flaking off, possibly on account of chemical treatment.
Ca. 4–6 cm from the entrance is a vertical row of four square holes, each ca. 4–5 × 4–5 cm.

figure.head: 83號窟平面圖。雷魯卡根據馬本漢和王平國田野繪圖製作
Ground plan of cave 83; drawing by Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

div.head: Text Markers

row.cell: [大般涅槃經聖行品之]二 十二 ( 十二 would have been smaller in size): no longer visible except for the second character 二 on the stone; the three characters 二, 十, and 二 are still discernable on the rubbing; reconstruction by analogy with cave 59, wall d, column 76; part 2 of chapter 7 (Shengxing pin 聖行品), coinciding with the beginning of scroll 12 of the Nirvana Sutra.
……五盛陰苦能生: the end of the engraving, midway through part 2 of chapter 7 (Shengxing pin 聖行品), in scroll 12 of the Nirvana Sutra.

div.p: Two graffiti characters, both reading guo 果, measuring 10 × 8.5 cm and 8.5 × 7 cm, are deeply incised, 123 and 125 cm from the floor, and 13 and 38 cm from the last column of the sutra.

div.head: Wall e
div.p: The striations on wall e are more methodical than those on wall d. On the lower part of the wall they slant to the right, and on the upper part they slant to the left.

div.head: Wall f
div.p: The rear part of wall f has the same coarse striations as those on the rear of wall d. The same is true of the ceiling, where the rear also differs from the front. Our conclusion is that scoring in the rear part of the cave was undertaken anew after the text at the front had already been engraved.

At a height of 100 cm above the floor and 8 cm from the entrance is a small irregular area, 15 × 8 cm, that was polished later, with an inscription in one column, 9.8 × 3.7 cm, reading: “Wang Bao kan 王寶龕.” The character bao has been purposefully damaged.
Size of characters: wang 王, 1.1 × 1.5 cm; kan 龕, 3.2 × 3.7 cm.

note.p: Peng Jiasheng gives the measurements of the cave and calculates its volume at 12.255 cubic meters. He writes that it is unclear which sutra was carved. He was given this information by Hu Wenhe.
Hu Wenhe gives the same dimensions of the cave as published in and repeats that it is unclear which sutra was carved. He adds, however, that ten columns of text have survived.
Hu Wenhe repeats the information in his earlier publication, .
The authors give the measurements of the cave. They note that no texts were carved on walls e and f. They provide measurements for the field of script on wall d, but do not identify the sutra text.

The editors of the Chengdu Municipal Institute of Cultural Relics and Archaeology give the measurements of the cave and a detailed description of its architecture. The text carved on wall d is initially misidentified as part of the Golden Light Sutra. In their table at the end of the volume, however, they identify the 18 columns of text as coming from part 2 of chapter 7 (Sacred Practice Chapter, Shengxing pin 聖行品) of the Nirvana Sutra, noting that the text is unfinished.
Lee includes cave 83 in her cluster VI, which corresponds to section F. She writes that chapters 10–14 of the Vimalakīrti Sutra were carved in this cave, and the remaining chapters in cave 85.

Cao and Yu give the measurements and surface area only for wall d. They write that the sutra text is almost illegible due to severe erosion, and record that only 18 columns of 91 characters each have survived, 1,638 characters in total.

The authors repeat the information in , but correctly identify the text as coming from the Nirvana Sutra. They give a full transcription of the sutra text, which they analyze with their own detailed scheme.
Hida describes the location of cave 83. She finds a sutra text carved on wall d, but does not identify it. She writes that although walls e and f have been polished, no sutra texts were carved.

Deng Xingliang gives the measurements and a general description of the cave. He records that only wall d is engraved, with a text in 20 columns from scroll 12 of the Nirvana Sutra. He transcribes the beginning and the end of the passage.
